page: Microsoft Azure FX24-6mds_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the FXmdsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with 6 vCPUs and 3 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C x86_64 processor operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The system stands out for its massive memory capacity of 504.0 GB, which translates to an exceptionally high resource density of 84.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Additionally, the instance includes 1417 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage options.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities. The server achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core testing with a score of 2362.0, and single-threaded gzip compression at 34.83 GB/sec. Conversely, multi-core performance metrics, such as the Geekbench multi-core score of 7259.0 and stress-ng multi-core score of 6660.14, fall into the average tier. This represents a clear hardware tradeoff, where single-threaded execution speed is prioritized over massive parallel processing power. Memory bandwidth is measured at 518.54 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 51.2 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

Given its extreme memory-to-vCPU ratio, the FX24-6mds_v2 is highly cost-efficient for workloads constrained by memory capacity rather than raw CPU core count. It is ideally suited for hosting large in-memory databases, high-throughput caching layers like Redis, and memory-bound enterprise applications. However, due to its limited core count, it is not recommended for highly parallelized compute tasks or large-scale LLM text generation.
description: Microsoft Azure FX24-6mds_v2 is an x86_64 memory-intensive virtual machine from the FXmdsv2 family, utilizing Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization. It features 6 vCPUs and 3 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, paired with an exceptionally large 504.0 GB of RAM and 1417 GB of local storage. This configuration delivers a high memory-to-core ratio of 84.0 GB per core. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core execution, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2362.0 and strong single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core performance remains average due to the lower core count. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that require high single-threaded performance rather than massive parallel compute. It is best suited for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX24-6mds_v2 is a memory-intensive VM featuring 6 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz, 504 GB of RAM, and 1417 GB of local storage for memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX24-6mds_v2 is a memory-intensive VM with 6 vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and 1417 GB local storage, optimized for in-memory databases.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring an exceptional memory-to-core ratio and high single-threaded performance for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz with 6 vCPUs
Equipped with 504.0 GB of RAM, providing 84.0 GB per core
Includes 1417 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and compression tests
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-bound workloads
